task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login with the provided admin credentials.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the Alert Profile creation section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Alert Profile', 'Create Alert', or equivalent alert configuration screen is open in the application.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert profile name is set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Profile Name or Alert Name text field contains exactly 'SSH Brute Force Detection'.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert severity is set to Critical.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The severity dropdown, radio button, or selection indicator is set to 'Critical'.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert criteria is configured to detect SSH failed logins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The log criteria, event filters, or rule selection shows configuration for SSH failed login attempts (e.g., selecting Linux/Unix logs, SSH protocol, and failed login event types).

task_completion.description: Verify the threshold and time window are set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold configuration shows '5' (or '> 5') occurrences/events, and the time window or duration is set to '5' minutes.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert profile was successfully saved or created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the alert was created, or the 'SSH Brute Force Detection' alert is visible in the list of active/saved alert profiles.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the ManageEngine web GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within a web browser showing the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer interface, not via terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual application form fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show actual dropdowns, text boxes, and buttons of the ManageEngine application being clicked and filled out, rather than text typed into a notepad or dummy page.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are open, and the sequence of screenshots shows a logical progression of filling out the form and saving it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The alert profile must be named exactly 'SSH Brute Force Detection'. The severity must be set to 'Critical'. The threshold must be set to trigger after 5 failed attempts. The time window must be set to 5 minutes.
